The Temple of Light is far enough away that the singing is but a distant sound, loud enough to perform its function but not loud enough to annoy Lucon and his neighbouring nobles. Lucon is the first heir of his father's estate, and is involved in Enetian politics as a councillor in the city's oligarchical senate. In the morning, he dresses in the traditional Mycean garb of a white tunic accented with a deep blue to signify his station, complete with leggings to resist the colder winds of the northern Cenerian climate. On his belt he attaches another sign of nobility, that of the curved sabre in a gilded sheath, proud of the fact that he is noble but also a citizen: allowed the honour of carrying a weapon within the city's walls.

Lucon begins his day by eating a respectable breakfast alongside his sisters, served by one of his slaves; inland fowl eggs and bean soup are a staple on the menu, as well as more exclusive seafood items such as swordfish, sturgeon roe, and octopus. His breakfast is taken at dawn, before he begins his training routine in archery. Nobles in other cities have a cavalry training regimen, a fact that Lucon envies. In Enetia, no space exists for horse corrals and large stables, much less open fields for practice. The noble Mycean tradition of the longbow, however, is practiced regularly and to a fault. He spends the rest of his morning swimming in the clean waters of Gold's Landing.

Before noon, Lucon sets off from his estate. His role as Sea Lord Amnon's adjutant requires regular meetings with captains of the merchant fleet. He eventually meets one unhappy petitioner, Galon of the Trading Fellowship, who has been refused rights to sail to The Eye for Haze transportation due to his lack of oversight with the delicate material. Many alchemists in the city have complained of shoddy and wet product on delivery by Galon, usually expected to be delivered in dry batches. They discuss business over a light lunch of wine, rare red meat, preserved fruits, and honeyed cakes while enjoying the view of pleasure craft docking and undocking at Gold's Landing, away from the hustle and bustle of the usual merchant docks. This is an honour in and of itself for Galon, who accepts rejection of his trade license in exchange for the gourmet meal and exclusive invitation to the district's best taverna, and for rubbing shoulders with one of the city's respected admirals' sons. Lucon assures him that, in time, Galon will be able to re-apply for the license.

Lucon's afternoon takes him to the Senary, where the Temple of Light which woke him at dawn is located. It is also where the city's council meets, and the location of his lord's office. Sea Lord Amnon is already chewing out some lesser officers when he arrives, giving him news that he decided against renewing Galon's Haze license. The Sea Lord only listens with one ear, preoccupied with the rising Thalen pirate threat ever since the Voyage of Normund saw many of Enetia's allies fall in the Golden Bay. Lucon is expected to attend meetings and give input (when requested) until the end of the day, when the council meets at sunset. Then, both he and Amnon are expected to attend a special session called for the navy's response to the rising pirate threat. Amnon gives a reasonable speech, but is heckled by some councillors for the lack of progress and lost shipments. The council adjourns when the last rays of the sun are cast, having come to no satisfactory conclusion on the matter.

Lucon sups with some of his fellow younger nobles in a Senary eatery, before they inevitably move on to bustling Arcshore, where the wine flows and his generation can let loose. Lucon's friend Denos visits his favourite prostitute, for which he is considered to have lost some honour, though his friends don't mind, and more importantly, don't tell. Lucon himself is courting a well-off merchant's daughter in the same district, who has yet to embrace his advances. Rejected again, Lucon heads to his home in Gold's Landing, alone. Away from the bustling crowds but before reaching the guards of the district, he is beset by some thugs, who have smuggled Thalen crossbows into the city (a dishonourable weapon). Sword drawn, Lucon realises he is outmatched, and is robbed of his fine clothes, sword, and whatever change of coin he carried that day. Left in only his leggings, he shakes off the shock of the robbery and decides to swim across the canal that separates Gold's Landing from Arcshore, sneaking past the guards who are sure to spread the news of his dishonour, something he is keen to avoid. He sneaks past a canal patrol and arrives, soggy and near-nude at his house, where his sisters tease him for his misfortune for the next season (though are secretly glad he is safe).

docs.title: A Merchant's Slice of Life
docs.text:
Pelica is a merchant of Aspola, first Mycean colony and city founded on Zinnabar. Her family have lived here for generations, and many were sailors, merchants, and artisans before her. Her own path was decided, when, like many Aspolans, she answered Andros' call to war during the Voyage of Normund. Her upbringing under her father, a merchant sailor, allowed Pelica to rise to master navigator aboard a Cenerian warship, a Dromon, which engaged Thalen warships near Movenna. After a disastrous boarding action, Pelica was wounded and thrown overboard, though Bel must have heard her prayers and let her unconscious body was ashore near Movenna, where she took refuge. The Sect of Pyos took her in, and was pleased with her merchant's ability in Arcanum, which she soon applied to scribal work in exchange for room and board. After some time, the war between Thalendor and Ceneria was ended, and Pelica was able to sail home aboard the once-again-sailing merchantmen.

Now living in Aspola again, Pelica captains her trading ship. She commands over one hundred rowers, her own navigators, and mercenary hirelings. Her trade is built on importing Haze to Aspola, where her brother is a renowned alchemist taught in the Enetian way of concocting the raw material into its final form. Unlike her life on the ship, a routine begins to form when she stays in the city. Pelica rises to the loud guttural singing of the Sect of Illumination which bathes the city in their dawn-song. Her mornings are spent eating bean soup and seafood such as sardines, mussels, and cod. Before noon, she is working on balancing her books using her elementary skills in Arcanum, or paying a visit to the docks to check on inventory and any loading operations. She owns one domestic slave, but many more work the warehouses which she co-owns as part of her membership of the Trading Fellowship. Her rowers, too, are slaves, though they are paid for by investments in her trading venture by other merchants.

docs.title: Haze
docs.text: The Haze is a corrupting ether that permeates many woods and mountain ranges of Osera and Hespenor. It is within the Haze that the Horrors live, and presumably multiply.

docs.title: Origin
docs.text: The sages of the Seventh Sanctum believe that the Haze is either caused by the Horrors, or alternatively that both are produced through some black magic by the Lethe. Alternatively, some plants may be responsible for producing the Haze, though these would have to be quite rugged since the Haze also hangs low over mountains. Further still, it may be a property of the land itself, as no Haze is known to exist in Zinnabar. Lastly, the abundance of Haze on The Eye suggests it is perhaps related to falling stars or other calamities which occurred during the Collapse. For the Sect of Illumination, it is Bel made incarnate.

docs.title: Properties
docs.text: The Haze is often described as similar to regular fog, but damper. When exposed to sunlight or fire, Haze eventually dries into powdery-crystal, perfect for transportation. On The Eye, Haze is so abundant that is has dried in layers, making it ripe for harvesting. In other areas, the dried Haze often mixes with dirt, mud, and other impurities instead, making it unprofitable for harvest. The Haze is often thought to enable the use of corrupted magic which can corrupt those exposed to it or who are not careful in its use. The sages of the Seventh Sanctum believe that Haze allow people to easily communicate with their Echoes, but also corrupts those Echoes at the same time.

docs.title: Trade
docs.text:
Concoctions of the Haze are sold on many streets of the cities of Osera, known simply as 'Haze', and are particularly sought after on Zinnabar where it has to be imported. Enetia has become the centre of Haze bottling, monopolising it as a trade secret. Enetia is particularly well-placed, as the Haze is weaker and stronger in certain areas, being strongest near The Eye, relatively close to Enetia.

The artificial bottling of Haze has resulted in a spike of supernatural abilities, something of great concern to the Seventh Sanctum whose scholars posit that disaster looms. They believe that the lack of oversight and training will inevitably result in a similar disaster as befell Thenea Epeson. For their part, many blame the Sixth Sanctum for the city's destruction and pay no heed to the sages' fear-mongering.

docs.title: Heavenly Iron
docs.text: A type of iron harvested from meteorites, able to be forged into a special steel called 'white steel' for its brilliant and mirror-like finish.

docs.title: Mycean powder
docs.text: Mycean powder is a combination of rockyellow (sulfur) and nitre, and is also known as "flame powder".

docs.title: Brethil-Thalen Conflict
docs.text: The original war between the Brethiln and Thalen peoples has largely subsided. What remains are border disputes and the slowly encroaching Thalen settlers on Brethil. This simmering tension has slowly been building up again due to the Thalen with the Cenerian League and lack of expansion options for its booming population.

docs.title: Thalen-Cenerian Conflict
docs.text: Though the peace has been signed in Arcanum, not everything is rosy. Cenerian priests of the Sect of Pyos are outraged that Thalen priests are now allowed in the city, and the recent election of the first Thalen Patriarch has nearly resulted, and may still result, in a schism. Beyond the religious conflict, Thalen pirates hound Enetian merchantmen and the Golden Bay.
